Chapter 03 — Pipelines / What Are Pipelines

What Are Pipelines

Foundation All Pipelines
How We Manage Leads and Clients

A sales pipeline is a structured system for tracking potential customers through a defined process — from first contact to a final outcome. Each person sits in a stage that represents exactly where they are in that process, and each stage tells the team what action is needed next.

We use pipelines to manage every lead and client from the moment they first make contact through to membership or close. Nothing gets lost, nothing sits idle, and the whole team can see the same picture at any time.

There are two pipelines at STRONG: the Leads Pipeline for new enquiries, and the Intro Offer Pipeline for clients who have purchased the Intro Offer or a promotional variant. Both live inside Hapana Grow as a Kanban board and run on the same principles. This section covers those principles. For the specifics of each pipeline, refer to the relevant section.

How the Kanban Board Works
The pipeline displays as a Kanban board — each column is a stage, and each person is a card. Clicking a card opens the client modal where you can view contact details, call notes, tasks, and automations.
TermWhat it means
StageA column on the board. Represents where the person is in the journey. Each stage has a defined action.
CardA person. One card per person per pipeline. Holds contact details, notes, tasks, and history.
StatusApplied when a card leaves the pipeline entirely. Appears at the bottom of the screen when dragging a card out. Default is Open.
Statuses
StatusWhen to use it
OpenDefault. Card is active — you are working this person.
WonPerson has purchased. Journey complete. System may apply this automatically.
AbandonedAll attempts exhausted or client has chosen not to continue. Soft no — may return in future.
LostHard no. Requested removal or not a fit (wrong number, out of area). Enables DND — blocks future automated messages.
Accessing Hapana Grow
Both pipelines live inside Hapana Grow — the CRM used across all STRONG locations. Log in at grow.hapana.com with your studio credentials. Speak to your manager if you don't have access.
🔗
Hapana Grow
grow.hapana.com
To access a pipeline: click Opportunities in the left nav → select the pipeline from the dropdown at the top of the board. You'll land on the Kanban view.
⚠️
For specific navigation steps for each pipeline — refer to the Accessing the Pipeline accordion inside each pipeline section.
Pipeline Hygiene
A pipeline is only useful if it reflects reality. Cards in the wrong stage, no tasks set, interactions not logged — these break the board for everyone. Pipeline hygiene is the discipline of keeping it accurate at all times.
RuleWhat it means
Move the card immediatelyAs soon as an outcome is clear — answered, booked, no answer, purchased — move the card. Don't leave it and come back later.
Always set a taskIf action is needed at a future date, set a task before you move on. No task means no follow-up. No follow-up means a lost opportunity.
Log a note every timeEvery call, SMS, and interaction gets a note. Any team member should be able to pick up a card and know exactly what happened and what's next.
No card sits idleIf a card has been in a stage longer than expected with no task set, something has been missed. Action it or close it out.
Close cards that are doneDragging to Abandoned or Lost is accuracy, not failure. A clean pipeline is faster to work and easier to trust.
⚠️
Stale cards slow everyone down. If you can't tell what happened or what's next from reading the notes — that's a hygiene failure. When in doubt: log a note, set a task, move the card.
Work Your Pipeline by Stage
Don't work the pipeline one card at a time from left to right. Work it by stage — action every card in a stage before moving to the next. Each stage requires a different mindset and a different action. Switching between them card by card fragments your focus and slows you down.
When you're in a stage, you're in that headspace only — calling new leads, or following up conversations, or checking in after a session. Finish the stage, then move to the next.
📋
The priority order for each pipeline is different. Refer to Work Your Pipeline by Stage inside each pipeline section for the specific order to follow each shift.
Note Format Standard
Every action gets a note. Notes are how the team stays aligned — anyone picking up a card should be able to read the notes and know exactly what happened and what's next without asking.
Standard Format
[action] — [outcome] — [next step or stage moved to]
Example NoteWhen to use it
call 1 — no answer — voicemail left — cna1 sms sentFirst call unanswered
call 2 — answered — follow up agreed — task setSpoke to lead, agreed callback
call 3 — no answer — final sms sent — dragged to abandonedFinal attempt, closing out
answered — purchased intro offer — dragged to wonSale made
welcome call — no answer — voicemail left — wcna sms sentIO Pipeline first call
💡
Keep it lowercase, brief, no full sentences. Hapana timestamps every note automatically — no need to add dates. Each stage in both pipelines has quick-copy note templates in its Notes tab — tap to copy, paste into Hapana.
Chapter 03 — Pipelines / Leads Pipeline

Leads Pipeline

Daily Use Sales Outreach
Understanding the Pipeline

The Leads Pipeline is where every new enquiry lives from the moment they first make contact through to their first purchase or close. The aim is to connect with every new enquiry, understand what they're looking for, and help them find the right way to get started. Speed on first contact and consistency on follow-up are what make the difference.

The goal of this pipeline is to get new leads started on the Intro Offer, Starter Class, or Casual session — in that order of priority. Intro Offer purchases will automatically move into the Intro Offer Pipeline where the customer nurture journey continues. Starter Class and casual visit bookings move to Appointment Booked — you'll follow up after their session to continue toward a longer commitment. Class pack and membership purchases are considered won and the lead is closed out.

Leads Pipeline Management
Open
Open the stage you're working.
Action
Follow the Action tab.
Note
Leave a note after every action or interaction.
Move
Move the card as soon as the outcome is clear.
Cadence at a Glance
COLLAPSE
Stage Focus Touchpoints
New Lead New enquiry — call immediately while interest is high Call 1 immediately · voicemail if no answer · CNA 1 SMS
Call No Answer 1 First call unanswered · second attempt +1 day / next shift · Call 2 · CNA 2 SMS if no answer
Call No Answer 2 Two calls unanswered · final attempt +1 day / next shift · Call 3 (final) · CNA 3 Final SMS → Abandoned if no answer
Active Conversation Lead is engaged · run full sales process Discovery → Options → Recommendation → Objections → Close
Follow Up Required Lead needs more time · keep momentum Call on task date · Follow Up SMS 1 if no answer / +1 day · Follow Up SMS 2 Final → Abandoned
Future Follow Up (holding) Timing not right · check monthly Check at start of each month · move to Follow Up Required if date reached
Appointment Booked Session booked · check in after class Call after session · Appt CNA 1 SMS if no answer / +1 day · Appt CNA 2 Final SMS → Abandoned
Sold Converted — pipeline complete Confirm purchase in Core · close as Won
Opt Out Lead no longer being pursued No action · closed
The Leads Pipeline lives inside Hapana Grow — the CRM used across all STRONG locations. To access it: open Hapana Grow → click Opportunities in the left nav → select Leads Pipeline from the pipeline dropdown at the top. You'll land on the Kanban board view.
🔗
Hapana Grow
grow.hapana.com — log in with your studio credentials. Speak to your manager if you don't have access.
Every lead in this pipeline sits in one of the stages below. Each stage has a specific meaning — it tells you and your team exactly where that lead is in the process and what action is needed next.
New Lead
CNA 1
CNA 2
CNA 3
Active Convo
Follow Up
Future F/U
Appt Booked
Sold
Opt Out
StageWhat it means
New LeadA new enquiry has come in. No contact made yet. Call immediately.
CNA 1First call attempt — no answer. SMS sent. Awaiting response or second attempt.
CNA 2Second call attempt — no answer. SMS sent. Awaiting response or third attempt.
CNA 3Third and final call attempt — no answer. Final SMS sent. Ready to close out.
Active ConversationTwo-way contact is happening — via call, SMS, or email. Lead is engaged.
Follow Up RequiredA specific follow-up has been agreed for this month. A task is set. Action on the due date.
Future Follow UpLead is interested but the timing isn't right — the agreed follow-up date is next month or beyond.
Appointment BookedLead has booked a Starter Class or casual visit. Follow up after their session.
SoldLead has purchased. Card stays here briefly before system or staff drags to Won.
Opt OutLead has opted out of communications on their end. Review account and move to appropriate stage or status.
Statuses
Statuses are applied when a card is dragged out of the pipeline entirely. They appear at the bottom of the screen when dragging. The default status is Open — all active pipeline cards are Open.
StatusWhen to use it
WonLead has purchased — membership, class pack, or Intro Offer. Journey complete.
AbandonedAll contact attempts exhausted with no response, or lead has opted out and been reviewed.
LostLead has requested removal or asked not to be contacted again.
OpenDefault. Card is active in the pipeline.
Every stage has a defined set of outcomes. When an outcome occurs, the card moves — no exceptions.
⚠️
Before You Move to Either of the Following Stages
  • Appointment Booked — confirm the booking is showing in Core and the client is appearing in the schedule before moving the card.
  • Dragging to Won — confirm the purchase has completed in Core and is not still sitting as pending before closing out.
OutcomeMove card to
Called — no answer (attempt 1)CNA 1 — send CNA 1 SMS
Called — no answer (attempt 2)CNA 2 — send CNA 2 SMS
Called — no answer (attempt 3)CNA 3 — send final SMS — drag to Abandoned
SMS reply received during CNA stageActive Conversation (or appropriate stage based on where the conversation ends)
Answered — continuing conversationActive Conversation or Follow Up Required (set task)
Answered — agreed follow-up dateFollow Up Required (set task for agreed date)
Agreed date is next month or beyondFuture Follow Up (set task for that date)
Booked Starter Class or casual visitAppointment Booked (set task for same date or day after to follow up session)
Purchased Intro OfferDrag to Won — system will move the opportunity to the IO Pipeline automatically
Purchased class pack or membershipDrag to Won — system may do this automatically, move manually if you catch it first
Lead has opted out of communicationsReview account in Grow and Core — move to appropriate stage or status
Requested removal / do not contactDrag to Lost
Non-Negotiables — Every Shift
Complete these every shift before anything else. If the pipeline is too large to work in full, start here — then rotate through the remaining stages one at a time across subsequent shifts.
StageWhy it can't wait
①  New LeadNew enquiry — call immediately while interest is highest.
②  Follow Up RequiredTask is due today or overdue — action every card with a due task.
③  Active ConversationLead is in dialogue — task is due today or overdue. Don't let it go cold.
④  Appointment BookedCall on the day of or day after their class. Miss the window and the lead goes cold.
Priority Rotation — One Stage Per Shift
After completing the non-negotiables, work the next stage on this list. Finish the stage completely before moving on. If your shift ends mid-stage, leave a handover note so the next staff member can complete it before moving to the next stage.
StageAction
⑤  CNA 1, 2, 3Work through call-no-answer leads in order. One attempt per stage per shift.
Monthly onlyFuture Follow Up — check at the start of every month only. Move any leads with a due date this month into Follow Up Required.
Example NoteWhen to use it
call 1 — no answer — voicemail left — cna1 sms sentFirst call unanswered
call 2 — answered — follow up agreed — task setSpoke to lead, agreed callback
call 3 — no answer — final sms sent — dragged to abandonedFinal attempt, closing out
answered — purchased intro offer — dragged to wonIO sale made
future follow up set — [month]Long-term interest — parked in Future F/U
💡
Quick-copy note templates are inside each stage below — tap any note to copy it directly into Hapana.
Discovery Notes
Before every call, copy the discovery notes template and paste it into the client's call notes field in Hapana — ready to fill in if they pick up. If they don't answer, save it blank for next time. Whenever you do speak to the lead and gather discovery information, fill it in and log a separate note to flag that it's been updated.
📋
Best practice: Paste the discovery template before you dial. Fill it in during or after the conversation. Log discovery notes updated as a separate note each time you add new information.
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes before you dial
New Lead
CNA 1
CNA 2
CNA 3
Active Convo
Follow Up
Future F/U
Appt Booked
Sold
Opt Out
01
New Lead
A new enquiry has come in. The lead is motivated right now — call immediately.
CALL NOW ▼
📼 Leave a voicemail if no answer at this stage
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Call the lead immediately — don't wait
  4. No answer → leave voicemail → send CNA 1 SMS → drag to Call No Answer 1
  5. Answer → run full sales process: Intro → Discovery → Options → Recommendation → Objections → Close
  6. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
CNA 1 SMS — Send if no answer on first call
VERSION A — Open question
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, it's {{custom_values.fromname_member}} at STRONG Pilates {{custom_values.location_short}}. Thanks for setting up an account, just tried to give you a quick welcome call. I was curious, what made you look into STRONG?
VERSION B — Closed question
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, it's {{custom_values.fromname_member}} at STRONG Pilates {{custom_values.location_short}}. Thanks for setting up an account, just tried to give you a quick welcome call. Are you looking to try out a class?
Voicemail
📼 Voicemail Script
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates. Thanks for setting up an account, just tried to give you a quick welcome call. I was curious about what made you look into STRONG or how we can help, please give me a call back when you get a chance, or feel free to reply to my text. Speak soon."
Call Script — First Contact (~10–15 min)
1
Intro ~30 sec
"Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates. Thanks for setting up your account with us — just wanted to give you a quick welcome call and see if you had any questions. I'm curious though, what made you look into STRONG?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
call 1 — no answer — voicemail left
call 1 — answered — booked intro offer
call 1 — answered — follow up
call 1 — answered — not interested — opted out
cna1 sms sent
moved to cna1
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
02
Call No Answer 1
First call went unanswered. Call again — this is your second attempt.
CALL AGAIN ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Call the lead — next shift after CNA 1
  4. No answer → send CNA 2 SMS → drag to Call No Answer 2
  5. Answer → run full sales process: Intro → Discovery → Options → Recommendation → Objections → Close
  6. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
CNA 2 SMS — Send if no answer
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, it's {{custom_values.fromname_member}}, STRONG — was just calling to check in and quickly follow up my last message, were you still looking at trying us out?
Call Script — First Contact
1
Intro
"Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates. Thanks for setting up your account with us — just wanted to give you a quick welcome call and see if you had any questions. I'm curious though, what made you look into STRONG?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
call 2 — no answer
call 2 — answered — booked intro offer
call 2 — answered — follow up
call 2 — answered — not interested — opted out
cna2 sms sent
moved to cna2
moved to active conversation — replied to sms
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
03
Call No Answer 2
Two calls unanswered. One final attempt before closing this lead out.
CALL AGAIN ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Call the lead — this is the final attempt
  4. No answer → send CNA 3 Final SMS → drag to Call No Answer 3, then immediately drag to Abandoned (no need to wait for next day)
  5. Answer → run full sales process: Intro → Discovery → Options → Recommendation → Objections → Close
  6. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
CNA 3 Final SMS — Send if no answer on final call
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} here from STRONG. I've tried to reach you a couple of times — no worries at all if the timing isn't right. If you'd like to chat about getting started, feel free to reply anytime. We'd love to have you. If not, we wish you all the best!

After this SMS, drag to Call No Answer 3, then immediately drag to Abandoned. If they reply at any point, revert to Open and move to the appropriate stage.

Call Script — First Contact
1
Intro
"Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates. Thanks for setting up your account with us — just wanted to give you a quick welcome call and see if you had any questions. I'm curious though, what made you look into STRONG?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.

Call Notes
call 3 — no answer — final sms sent
call 3 — answered — booked intro offer
call 3 — answered — follow up
call 3 — answered — not interested — opted out
cna3 final sms sent — moved to cna3 — dragged to abandoned
moved to active conversation — replied to sms
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
04
Call No Answer 3
Three calls made with no answer — drag to Abandoned. If they respond, revert to Open.
FINAL CALL ▼
What to do
  1. Drag client card to Abandoned
  2. Add a note
  3. The final CNA SMS was already sent at CNA 2 — if they respond at any point, revert their status back to Open and move to the appropriate stage
  4. To revert: open client account screen → find Opportunity Updated in the activity feed → click View Opportunity → change Status from Abandoned to Open → change Stage to appropriate stage → click Update
🚫
No SMS for this stage. The final SMS was already sent at CNA 2. If the lead responds, revert to Open and move to the appropriate stage.
Call Script — If They Respond and You Call Back
1
Intro
"Hi [Name], this is [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates. Thanks for setting up your account with us — just wanted to give you a quick welcome call and see if you had any questions. I'm curious though, what made you look into STRONG?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
cna3 — moved to abandoned
replied after cna3 — reverted to open — moved to active conversation
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
05
Active Conversation
The lead has responded — you're in dialogue. Keep the conversation going and help them find the right next step.
ENGAGED ▼
This lead is talking to you. They came from CNA or replied to an SMS. Keep the conversation warm and moving. Your goal is to keep them engaged and help them move forward — call or SMS, wherever they're responding.
What to do
  1. Respond promptly — don't let conversations go cold
  2. Continue the conversation wherever the customer is responding — if moving to a call would help progress things more fluidly or efficiently, aim for that. But SMS is a valid channel and sometimes the right one
  3. If calling: use the re-entry opener, then continue the sales process from wherever you left off
  4. Not interested → apply Abandoned or Lost status based on their reason
  5. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
💬
No template SMS for this stage. Respond naturally to the conversation in progress. Your goal is to get them on a call or booked in.
Re-entry Opener
1
Re-entry Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG — just calling to follow up. From our messages / last time we spoke, [brief summary of what you covered]. [Continue from wherever you are in the sales process — Discovery, Options, Recommendation, or Close.]"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
active conversation — following up
called — no answer — moved to follow up required
answered — booked intro offer — moved to sold
answered — class booked — moved to appointment booked
answered — follow up — moved to follow up required
not interested — abandoned or lost status applied
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
06
Follow Up Required
You've spoken and a follow-up has been agreed for this month. Action on the due date.
CALL AGAIN ▼
What to do
  1. Check your task — call on the agreed date or as soon as your task is due
  2. Review discovery notes before calling — know where you left off
  3. Call the lead using the re-entry opener
  4. No answer → send Follow Up SMS Attempt 1 → set task for next shift
  5. Second attempt — no answer → send Follow Up SMS Attempt 2 Final → drag to Abandoned
  6. Reply received at any point → move to Active Conversation
  7. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
Follow Up SMS Attempt 1 — Send if no answer on first follow-up call
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG — just tried to give you a call to follow up from our last chat. Feel free to call or text back whenever suits you. Otherwise I'll try again soon.
Follow Up SMS Attempt 2 Final — Send if no answer on second follow-up call
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} here from STRONG. Just a quick final follow up — if the timing isn't right, no worries at all. But if you're ready to get started or want to continue the chat, just reach out. All the best.

After the final SMS with no response, drag to Opt Out.

Re-entry Opener
1
Re-entry Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG — just calling to follow up. Last time we spoke we talked about [brief summary]. Just wanted to pick up from there."
Then → branch based on where you're up to
If discovery isn't complete: "I was also meaning to ask — [next discovery question]"
If discovery done: go straight into options.
If options/rec already covered: "I know you were thinking it over between [option A] and [option B] — which way were you mostly leaning towards?" → Then close
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"

"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"

"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"

Call Notes
follow up call — no answer — sms sent
follow up call — answered — booked — moved to won

follow up call — answered — future date — moved to future follow up

Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
07
Future Follow Up
Lead is interested but the timing isn't right — the agreed follow-up date is next month or beyond. Check at the start of every month.
HOLDING ▼
Monthly task: At the start of every month, check this stage. Any leads whose agreed follow-up date falls in the current month must be moved to Follow Up Required and actioned before month end.
What to do
  1. Set a task for the agreed follow-up date and leave the lead here until then
  2. When the date arrives: move to Follow Up Required and action as per that stage
  3. Monthly check: first of every month — scan this stage for current month dates
📅
No SMS for this stage. The lead is in a holding pattern — a future date has been agreed. When that date arrives, move to Follow Up Required and action from there.
Re-entry Opener — When Date Arrives
1
Re-entry Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG — just calling to follow up. I know it's been a little while — last time we spoke we talked about [brief summary]. Just wanted to pick up from there."
Then → branch based on where you're up to
If discovery isn't complete: "I was also meaning to ask — [next discovery question]"
If discovery done: go straight into options.
If options/rec already covered: "I know you were thinking it over between [option A] and [option B] — which way were you mostly leaning towards?" → Then close
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"

"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"

"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"

Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
future follow up set
moved to follow up required — date reached
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
08
Appointment Booked
Lead has booked a class but hasn't purchased the Intro Offer. Check in after their session — find out how it went and help them take the next step.
CLASS BOOKED ▼
This lead has booked a class (casual, Starter Class, free class, or community class) but has not yet purchased the Intro Offer. The goal is to connect after their session, hear how they found it, and move towards the Intro Offer, or help them decide what's right for them.
What to do
  1. Set a follow-up task for the day of their class
  2. Call after their session or the next day — use the check-in opener
  3. Listen to how they found it, then move into the sales process toward the Intro Offer
  4. No answer → send CNA 1 SMS → set task for next shift
  5. Second attempt — no answer → send CNA 2 Final SMS → drag to Abandoned
  6. If applicable, drag card to the appropriate stage → set task if required
CNA 1 SMS — Send if no answer after their class
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG. Was calling to check in on your class, how was your experience?
CNA 2 Final SMS — Send if no answer on second attempt
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} here. Haven't been able to connect — hope the class went well! Just a quick final check in — were you still interested in continuing with STRONG? Would love to have you, if not, no worries at all, reach out when you're ready. All the best.

After final SMS with no response, drag to Opt Out.

Post-Session Check-In Opener
1
Post-Session Check-In
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG — just calling to check in on how your session went."
Listen to how they found it. Acknowledge. If discovery isn't complete, fill the gaps naturally. Then move into the Intro Offer recommendation.
"That's great to hear — based on what you've told me about [goal/what they shared], I think the Intro Offer is the perfect next step. It gives you [X sessions / X days] to really experience what STRONG can do for you."
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1 already asked in the intro — keep listening before moving on. Work through Q2–Q5 naturally.
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
Standard leadCasual vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
BeginnerStarter Class vs Intro Offer (or current promo)
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"From these options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.

Call Notes
post-class call — no answer — sms sent
post-class call — answered — purchased intro offer — moved to sold
post-class call — answered — follow up
post-class call — answered — not interested — abandoned or lost status applied
final sms sent — no response — dragged to abandoned
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
09
Sold
Lead has purchased the Intro Offer. Move this card to the Intro Offer Pipeline in Hapana.
CONVERTED ▼
Great work — they're in. The lead has purchased the Intro Offer. Your next job is to hand them off to the Intro Offer Pipeline and make sure their first session is locked in.
What to do
  1. Confirm the purchase is processed in Hapana
  2. Move this card to the Intro Offer Pipeline in Hapana
  3. Ensure their first class is booked
  4. If first class is not yet booked, help them find a suitable time before ending the call
🎉
No outbound SMS needed at this stage. The client has purchased — move them to the Intro Offer Pipeline and ensure their first class is booked.
✅
No script needed here. Sale is done. Focus on confirming the booking and handing off to the IO Pipeline. See Section 06 — Intro Offer Pipeline for what happens next.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
purchased intro offer — moved to io pipeline
first class booked: [date/time]
10
Opt Out
Lead has opted out of communications — review the account and move to the appropriate stage or status.
CLOSED ▼
What to do
  1. Cards may land here automatically when a lead opts out of communications — or you may identify during account review that a card belongs here
  2. Review the account in Grow and Core
  3. Move the card to the appropriate stage or status based on what you find
  4. If they reach back out at any point, move to Active Conversation and continue from there
🚫
No outbound SMS for this stage. This lead has been closed out. If they reach out, move to Active Conversation.
🚫
No outbound calls for this stage. This lead has been closed out. If they reach out, move to Active Conversation.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
opted out — no further action
all attempts exhausted — no response — closed
re-engaged — moved to active conversation
Chapter 03 — Pipelines / Intro Offer Pipeline

Intro Offer Pipeline

Daily Use Nurture & Convert
Understanding the Pipeline

The Intro Offer Pipeline is where every intro client lives from the moment they purchase through to conversion or close. This includes the standard Intro Offer and any promotional variants.

The aim is to get clients into their first session quickly, support them through the trial, complete discovery, and guide them to a membership or class pack that fits their goals and frequency. Clients who convert are moved to Sold and closed as Won. Clients who don't respond after all attempts or choose not to continue are moved to Abandoned or Lost.

Intro Offer Pipeline Management
Open
Open the stage you're working.
Action
Follow the Action tab.
Note
Leave a note after every action or interaction.
Move
Move the card as soon as the outcome is clear.
Cadence at a Glance
COLLAPSE
Stage Focus Touchpoints
Purchase Welcome them · get them booked and started Call 1 · voicemail if no answer · WCNA SMS
Welcome Call Complete (holding stage) Hapana drives the next move No action
NA 4–13 Days (auto-move day 4) Still motivated — guide them to book in No Attend Call 1 · voicemail · No Attend 1 SMS / +1 day · No Attend Call 2 · no voicemail · No Attend 2 SMS
NA >14 Days (auto-move day 14) Final outreach · last chance to get them started Call 4 · no voicemail · NA >14 Days Final SMS · set task 2 weeks / Task date → check booking → abandon if still nothing
1st Visit Complete (auto-move on attendance) How did it go · service follow-up · guide to next booking Call · voicemail if no answer · 1st Visit CNA SMS
Day 2–11 (auto-move daily) Keep momentum · encourage consistent booking ITNB 1 SMS · snooze Recovr 3 days / +3 days · ITNB 2 SMS if still not booked · no calls
Day 12 / Class 3–5 (auto-move) How's it all going · conversion starts In-person preferred · single call if not spoken in person · full conversion conversation
Expired (auto-move) Understand their experience · conversion Call 1 · voicemail · ENA1 SMS / +1 day · Call 2 · no voicemail / +1 day · Call 3 · no voicemail · ENA3 Final SMS → Abandoned
Future Follow Up Long game · stay connected Call on task date · IOFUP 1 SMS if no answer / +1 day · IOFUP 2 Final SMS → Abandoned
The Intro Offer Pipeline lives inside Hapana Grow. To access it: open Hapana Grow → click Opportunities in the left nav → select Intro Offer Pipeline from the pipeline dropdown at the top. You'll land on the Kanban board view.
🔗
Hapana Grow
grow.hapana.com — log in with your studio credentials. Speak to your manager if you don't have access.
StageWhat it meansHow cards move
PurchaseClient has purchased. Welcome call to be made immediately.Manual — staff work this stage on purchase
Welcome Call CompleteWelcome call made. Holding stage — Hapana drives the next move.Auto-move on day 4 (no attendance) or on first class attendance
No Attendance 4–13 DaysClient hasn't attended in 4–13 days from purchase. Two call attempts to get them booked.Auto-move at day 4 from purchase
No Attendance >14 DaysNo attendance after 14+ days. Final outreach before Abandoned.Auto-move at day 14 from purchase
1st Visit CompleteClient attended their first class. Check in and keep momentum going.Auto-move on first class attendance
Day 2–14Client is active in their trial. Daily Recovr check — monitor booking status.Auto-move daily through trial period
Day 12 / Class 3–5Conversion window. Discovery complete — conversion conversation.Auto-move at Day 12 or Class 3–5
ExpiredIntro Offer has expired without conversion. Three call attempts.Auto-move on IO expiry
Expired (Call No Answer 1–3)No answer sub-stages for the expired conversion attempts.Manual — staff drag after each unanswered call
Future Follow UpClient requested follow up at a later date. Task set.Manual — staff drag when appropriate
Membership / PackageClient has converted. Pipeline complete — Won.Auto-move on purchase of membership or pack
Most moves in this pipeline are automatic — Hapana tracks attendance and days since purchase and moves cards accordingly. The manual moves are yours to action.
TriggerMove toWho moves it
Client purchases Intro OfferPurchaseAuto
Welcome call made (answer or no answer)Welcome Call CompleteStaff — drag after call
4 days from purchase, no attendanceNo Attendance 4–13 DaysAuto
14 days from purchase, no attendanceNo Attendance >14 DaysAuto
Client attends first class1st Visit CompleteAuto
Day 2 onward through trialDay 2–14 (daily)Auto — moves each day
Day 12 reached or Class 3–5 completedDay 12 / Class 3–5Auto
Intro Offer expiresExpiredAuto
Expired call unansweredExpired CNA 1 / 2 / 3Staff — drag after each unanswered call
CNA 3 → ENA3 Final SMS sentExpired CNA 3 → immediately AbandonedStaff — drag both in sequence
Client convertsMembership / PackageAuto on purchase
All attempts exhausted, no responseAbandonedStaff — drag
Client requests follow up laterFuture Follow UpStaff — drag, set task date
ℹ️
14-day expiry clock — triggered by the first class booking date, not purchase date. Cancelling or no-showing the first booking does NOT reset the clock.
Non-Negotiables — Every Shift
Complete these every shift before anything else. If the pipeline is too large to work in full, start here — then rotate through the remaining stages one at a time across subsequent shifts.
StageWhy it can't wait
①  PurchaseNew purchase — welcome call immediately while motivation is highest.
②  Future Follow UpTask due today or overdue only — action every card with a due task.
③  1st Visit CompleteClient just had their first session — call same day or next shift. Window closes fast.
④  Day 2–11 Recovr CheckSMS only — fast to action. Check every client without an upcoming booking and send the appropriate SMS. A client not booking during trial will not convert.
Priority Rotation — One Stage Per Shift
After completing the non-negotiables, work the next stage on this list. Finish the stage completely before moving on. If your shift ends mid-stage, leave a handover note so the next staff member can complete it before moving to the next stage.
StageAction
⑤  Day 12 / Class 3–5Conversion window — one call, one chance. Don't let this stage sit.
⑥  ExpiredIO has expired without conversion — three call attempts to recover.
⑦  Expired CNA 1, 2, 3Follow-up attempts for expired clients who didn't answer. Work in order.
⑧  NA 4–13 DaysStill within trial window and motivated — get them booked in.
⑨  NA >14 DaysFinal outreach — one last attempt before closing out.
📋
In-person counts as a call. If you've spoken to the client face-to-face before or after a session, that interaction counts as the touchpoint for that stage — you don't need to call as well. Always leave an interaction note regardless.
Example NoteWhen to use it
welcome call — no answer — voicemail left — wcna sms sentPurchase stage — no answer
welcome call — answered — booked [date] — moved to wc completePurchase stage — answered, booking confirmed
na call — no answer — no attend 1 sms sentNA 4–13 Days — first attempt
expired call — answered — conversion convo — purchased [product]Expired — converted
expired call — no answer — ena1 sms sentExpired — no answer first attempt
d12 convo — in person — interested — follow up [date]Day 12 / Class 3–5 — in-person interaction
future follow up — task set [month]Client requested follow up later
💡
Quick-copy note templates are inside each stage's Notes tab. No dates needed — Hapana timestamps automatically.
Purchase
WC Complete
NA 4–13
NA >14
1st Visit
Day 2–11
D12 / C3–5
Expired
Exp CNA 1
Exp CNA 2
Exp CNA 3
Future F/U
Sold
01
Purchase
Client has purchased the Intro Offer. Welcome call to be made immediately.
CALL NOW ▼
📼 Leave a voicemail if no answer at this stage
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Check if client has a class booked — Hapana Core Schedule or Recovr
  4. Call the client — welcome them to STRONG
  5. No answer → leave voicemail → send WCNA SMS (select correct version) → drag to Welcome Call Complete
  6. Answer + not booked → complete discovery · help book first session · drag to Welcome Call Complete
  7. Answer + already booked → complete discovery · drag to Welcome Call Complete
  8. Drag client card to appropriate stage if applicable → set task if required
WCNA — No Class Booked
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} at STRONG Pilates calling, sorry I missed you. I can see you've purchased the STRONG Intro Offer, just here to help you get started with booking your classes - any questions you can reach me here. Will try again soon if you haven't already booked in for a session.
WCNA — Class Already Booked
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} at STRONG Pilates calling, sorry I missed you. I can see you've booked in your first class on the STRONG Intro Offer and just wanted to give you a quick welcome call. Any questions please reach out, looking forward to seeing you in studio.
Voicemail
📼 Voicemail Script — No Class Booked
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates — just tried to give you a quick welcome call. I'm here to answer any questions and help you get your first session booked in. Give me a call back when you get a chance or feel free to reply to my text. Speak soon."
📼 Voicemail Script — Class Already Booked
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates — just tried to give you a quick welcome call. I can see you've already got your first class booked in, which is awesome. Looking forward to seeing you in studio — give me a call back if you have any questions. Speak soon."
Call Script — Welcome Call
1
Opener — No Class Booked
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just a quick welcome call to say thanks for purchasing the Intro Offer, and I'm calling to answer any questions you might have and to help you book in for your first session. Just wondering, were you looking at an AM or PM class?"
If discovery not done → go into discovery. Guide them to book via app or book for them.
1
Opener — Class Already Booked
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just a quick welcome call to say thanks for purchasing the Intro Offer. I can see you've already got your first class booked in, which is great. Do you have any questions before your first session?"
If discovery not done → go into discovery. Answer questions, build anticipation, wrap up.
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
📅
Conversion starts later. Focus here is completing discovery and getting them booked. The full sales process — options, recommendation and close — begins at Day 12 / Class 3–5 or Expired.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
welcome call — no answer — voicemail left — wcna sms sent — moved to wc complete
welcome call — answered — first class booked — moved to wc complete
welcome call — answered — class already booked — discovery complete — moved to wc complete
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
02
Welcome Call Complete
Welcome call has been made. Holding stage — Hapana drives the next move.
HOLDING ▼
About this stage
The welcome call has been made. This is a holding stage — no action required. Hapana will automatically move the client to the appropriate stage based on attendance and days since purchase.
📞
No script required for this stage. Hapana drives the next move automatically.
💬
No SMS for this stage. Client will be contacted again when Hapana moves their card.
📝
No action required. Notes were logged at the Purchase stage.
03
No Attendance (4–13 Days)
Client purchased but hasn't attended. Check booking status — two call attempts if not booked.
CALL AGAIN ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Check if client has a class booked — Hapana Core Schedule or Recovr
If already booked
Check notes — did they answer the welcome call?
  1. Yes → set task for date of first session · no further action needed
  2. No → call the client · complete discovery if not done · set task for date of class or day after
If not booked
Call 2 First attempt — call immediately Immediate
  1. Call the client
  2. No answer → leave voicemail → send No Attend 1 SMS → mark WC No Answer 2
  3. Answer → complete discovery if not done · help book first session · set task based on outcome
  4. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
Call 3 Second attempt — still not booked +1 day / next shift
  1. Call the client — no voicemail
  2. No answer → send No Attend 2 SMS → mark WC No Answer 3
  3. Answer → complete discovery if not done · help book first session · set task based on outcome
  4. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
No Attend 1 SMS — Send after No Attend Call 1 if no answer
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} at STRONG, quick follow up, just noticed you haven't booked in yet after purchasing the Intro Offer. Were you okay with getting booked in, or would you like a class recommendation to start? Let me know.
No Attend 2 SMS — Send after No Attend Call 2 if no answer
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG again. I've tried a few times to reach you but haven't heard back yet. Hope everything's okay. Were you still looking to book in?
Voicemail
📼 Voicemail Script — No Attend Call 1
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates — just tried to give you a call to check in. I noticed you haven't booked in for your first session yet — please give me a call back when you get a chance or reply to my text and I'll help with getting you booked in. Speak soon."
Call Script — NA 4–13 Days (NA Call 1 & NA Call 2)
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just calling to check in, I noticed you haven't booked in for your first session yet. Did you want me to help you find a time that works?"
Discovery not done → discovery first, then help book. Discovery done → help book directly.
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
📅
Conversion starts later. Focus here is completing discovery and getting them booked. The full sales process — options, recommendation and close — begins at Day 12 / Class 3–5 or Expired.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
no attend call 1 — no answer — voicemail left — no attend 1 sms sent — wc no answer 2 marked
no attend call 2 — no answer — no voicemail — no attend 2 sms sent — wc no answer 3 marked
call — answered — first session booked — task set
already booked — task set for first session date
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
04
No Attendance (>14 Days)
No attendance after 14+ days. Final call attempt before abandoning.
FINAL CALL ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — paste discovery template if not already there
  3. Call the client — this is the final attempt · no voicemail
  4. No answer → send NA >14 Days Final SMS · set task for 2 weeks
  5. Answer → complete discovery if not done · help book first session · set task based on outcome
  6. Task date reached → check if client has booked
  7. No booking → drag to Abandoned
  8. Booked → set task for date of class or day after · card stays in this stage until Hapana auto-moves on attendance
NA >14 Days Final SMS — Send if no answer
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG. It's been a couple of weeks since you purchased the Intro Offer. We're excited to see you in studio and really hope to see you soon. Just thought we'd check in one last time in case you needed any help with bookings or had any questions. Please contact us or book in whenever you're ready.
Call Script — NA >14 Days
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just a quick welcome call, it's been a couple of weeks since you purchased the Intro Offer and I wanted to check in. Was something stopping you from getting started, or were you happy for me to help you get your first class booked in?"
Discovery not done → discovery first, then help book. Discovery done → help book directly.
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
📅
Conversion starts later. Focus here is completing discovery and getting them booked. The full sales process — options, recommendation and close — begins at Day 12 / Class 3–5 or Expired.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
call 4 — no answer — no voicemail — na>14 final sms sent — task set 2 weeks
call 4 — answered — first session booked — task set for class date
task date reached — no booking — moved to abandoned
task date reached — class now booked — task reset for class date
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
05
1st Visit Complete
Client has attended their first class. Check in, keep momentum going.
CALL TODAY ▼
📼 Leave a voicemail if no answer at this stage
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Call the client same day after their session · or next shift if not possible
  3. No answer → leave voicemail → send 1st Visit CNA SMS
  4. Answer → check in on how their session went · complete discovery if not done · run options, recommendation and close
  5. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
1st Visit CNA SMS — Send if no answer
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG. Was calling to check in on your first class, how was your experience?
Voicemail
📼 Voicemail Script
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates — just tried to give you a call to check in on your first class. Hope it went well! Give me a call back when you get a chance or feel free to reply to my text. Speak soon."
Call Script — 1st Visit Complete
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just calling to check in on your first class. How did you go?"
Listen to their response. If positive → complete discovery if not done → help book next session → move into options and recommendation if appropriate.
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
📅
Conversion starts later. Focus here is completing discovery and getting them booked. The full sales process — options, recommendation and close — begins at Day 12 / Class 3–5 or Expired.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
1st visit call — no answer — voicemail left — 1st visit cna sms sent
1st visit call — answered — positive feedback — discovery complete — next session booked
1st visit call — answered — converted — membership purchased — moved to sold
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
06
Day 2–11
Client is active in trial. Daily Recovr check — keep momentum, encourage consistent booking.
RECOVR CHECK ▼
What to do
  1. Check Recovr — conversion column shows who is not booked
  2. Booked → no action needed
  3. Not booked → check Recovr — has ITNB 1 SMS been sent?
  4. No → send ITNB 1 SMS → log note in Hapana → snooze Recovr card 3 days
  5. ITNB 1 already sent → check — has ITNB 2 SMS been sent?
  6. No → send ITNB 2 SMS → log note in Hapana
  7. Both sent → no further action · next contact is Day 12 / Class 3–5
📞
No outbound calls during this period — unless the client reaches out or a call is clearly required. Use your judgement — sometimes a call is the easiest way to reach someone. SMS is the standard approach for this stage.
📞
No standard call script for this stage. If a call is needed, check in on how the trial is going and encourage them to book their next session.
ITNB 1 SMS — Send when not booked (first attempt)
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, STRONG {{custom_values.fromname_member}}, tried calling to check in on how you're going so far. Please book in for your next session or let me know if you have any questions, keen to see you again in studio.
ITNB 2 SMS — Send +3 days later if still not booked
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, STRONG {{custom_values.fromname_member}}, quick reminder to book in a class before your Intro Offer expires, would you like me to help you book in for your next session or are you all good?
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Notes
recovr check — not booked — itnb 1 sms sent — recovr snoozed 3 days
recovr check — still not booked — itnb 2 sms sent
recovr check — booked — no action required
07
Day 12 / Class 3–5
Conversion window. Discovery first if not done — then full conversion conversation.
CONVERSION ▼
What to do
  1. Check Recovr — review booking status and any previous interactions
  2. Check notes — has discovery been completed?
  3. Call the client on Day 12 or Session 3–5
  4. No answer → no voicemail · no SMS · no further action until Expired
  5. Answer + discovery not done → complete discovery · run options, recommendation and close
  6. Answer + discovery done → run options, recommendation and close
  7. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
🤝
In-person preferred. If the client is in studio around this time, have the conversion conversation face-to-face — it's the most natural and effective approach. Still make the call if you haven't spoken in person.
Call Script — Day 12 / Class 3–5
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — just calling to check in on how your Intro Offer is going. How have the sessions been feeling?"
Positive → bridging toolkit below → options, recommendation and close. Negative or objection → refer to Chapter 4 — Objections & Scenarios. Discovery not done → complete discovery first.
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Done
Use open momentum-building questions only. No closed yes/no questions. Reference what they told you during discovery.
"You mentioned you wanted to do [X] sessions a week — if you were to continue, were you leaning towards [product] or [product]?"
"Based on everything you've told me, it sounds like [goal] is still the priority — how are you feeling about continuing to work towards that?"
"You've done [X] sessions now — from what you've experienced, do you feel like this is something you want to keep going with?"
"On a scale of 1–10, where are you sitting with continuing after the Intro Offer?"
"From what you've told me about [goal] and wanting to train [X] times a week — does continuing make sense for you?"
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Not Done
Complete discovery first, then use these questions to bridge to conversion.
"Roughly on a scale of 1–10, where would you say you sit with continuing after your Intro Offer?"
"How are you feeling about continuing?"
"From what you've experienced so far, do you feel like STRONG is the right fit for that?"
"If you were to keep this going, how many sessions a week do you think you'd realistically do?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
2/week2/Week Membership vs Class Pack
3+/weekUnlimited Membership vs 2/Week Membership
FlexibleClass Pack vs 2/Week Membership
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"Of those two options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your conversion
💬
No SMS for this stage. Call only — no voicemail, no SMS if no answer.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
d12/c3-5 call — no answer — no voicemail — no sms
d12/c3-5 call — answered — discovery complete — conversion conversation — follow up set
d12/c3-5 — in person conversation — discovery complete — conversion discussed
converted — membership purchased — moved to sold
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
08
Expired
Intro Offer has expired without conversion. Check in on their experience and move into conversion.
CALL NOW ▼
📼 Leave a voicemail if no answer at this stage
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — review discovery and any previous interactions
  3. Call the client — check in on their experience · move into conversion if the moment is right
  4. No answer → leave voicemail → send ENA1 SMS → drag to Expired (Call No Answer 1)
  5. Answer → check in on experience · complete discovery if not done · run options, recommendation and close
  6. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
ENA1 SMS — Send after Call 1 if no answer
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG pilates - sorry we missed you. Wanted to check in on how your Intro Offer experience was. How have the sessions felt for you so far?
Voicemail
📼 Voicemail Script
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] calling from STRONG Pilates — just tried to give you a call to check in on how your Intro Offer experience was. Give me a call back when you get a chance or feel free to reply to my text. Speak soon."
Call Script — Expired
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — wanted to check in on how your Intro Offer experience was. How have the sessions felt for you so far?"
Positive → bridging toolkit below → options, recommendation and close. Negative or objection → refer to Chapter 4 — Objections & Scenarios. Discovery not done → complete discovery first.
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Done
Use open momentum-building questions only. No closed yes/no questions. Reference what they told you during discovery.
"You mentioned you wanted to do [X] sessions a week — if you were to continue, were you leaning towards [product] or [product]?"
"Based on everything you've told me, it sounds like [goal] is still the priority — how are you feeling about continuing to work towards that?"
"You've done [X] sessions now — from what you've experienced, do you feel like this is something you want to keep going with?"
"On a scale of 1–10, where are you sitting with continuing after the Intro Offer?"
"From what you've told me about [goal] and wanting to train [X] times a week — does continuing make sense for you?"
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Not Done
Complete discovery first, then use these questions to bridge to conversion.
"Roughly on a scale of 1–10, where would you say you sit with continuing after your Intro Offer?"
"How are you feeling about continuing?"
"From what you've experienced so far, do you feel like STRONG is the right fit for that?"
"If you were to keep this going, how many sessions a week do you think you'd realistically do?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
2/week2/Week Membership vs Class Pack
3+/weekUnlimited Membership vs 2/Week Membership
FlexibleClass Pack vs 2/Week Membership
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"Of those two options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.

expired call 1 — no answer — voicemail left — ena1 sms sent — moved to exp cna 1

Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
09
Expired (Call No Answer 1)
First call went unanswered. Second attempt — no voicemail.
CALL AGAIN ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. +1 day / next shift → call the client — no voicemail
  3. No answer → drag to Expired (Call No Answer 2) · no SMS
  4. Answer → check in on experience · complete discovery if not done · run options, recommendation and close
  5. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
Call Script — Expired CNA 1 / CNA 2 / CNA 3
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — wanted to check in on how your Intro Offer experience was. How have the sessions felt for you so far?"
Positive → bridging toolkit below → options, recommendation and close. Negative or objection → refer to Chapter 4 — Objections & Scenarios. Discovery not done → complete discovery first.
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Done
Use open momentum-building questions only. No closed yes/no questions. Reference what they told you during discovery.
"You mentioned you wanted to do [X] sessions a week — if you were to continue, were you leaning towards [product] or [product]?"
"Based on everything you've told me, it sounds like [goal] is still the priority — how are you feeling about continuing to work towards that?"
"You've done [X] sessions now — from what you've experienced, do you feel like this is something you want to keep going with?"
"On a scale of 1–10, where are you sitting with continuing after the Intro Offer?"
"From what you've told me about [goal] and wanting to train [X] times a week — does continuing make sense for you?"
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Not Done
Complete discovery first, then use these questions to bridge to conversion.
"Roughly on a scale of 1–10, where would you say you sit with continuing after your Intro Offer?"
"How are you feeling about continuing?"
"From what you've experienced so far, do you feel like STRONG is the right fit for that?"
"If you were to keep this going, how many sessions a week do you think you'd realistically do?"
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"

6
Close

"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.

Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
💬
No SMS for this stage. If no answer, drag to Expired CNA 2.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
expired call 2 — no answer — no voicemail — no sms — moved to exp cna 2
expired call 2 — answered — conversion conversation — follow up set
converted — membership purchased — moved to sold
10
Expired (Call No Answer 2)
Two calls unanswered. Final attempt — ENA3 Final SMS if no answer, then Abandoned.
FINAL CALL ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. +1 day / next shift → call the client — no voicemail
  3. No answer → send ENA3 Final SMS → drag to Expired (Call No Answer 3) → immediately drag to Abandoned
  4. Answer → check in on experience · complete discovery if not done · run options, recommendation and close
  5. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
Call Script — same opener as Expired CNA 1
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG Pilates — wanted to check in on how your Intro Offer experience was. How have the sessions felt for you so far?"
Positive → bridging toolkit below → options, recommendation and close. Negative or objection → refer to Chapter 4 — Objections & Scenarios. Discovery not done → complete discovery first.
2
Bridging Toolkit — Discovery Done
Use open momentum-building questions only. No closed yes/no questions. Reference what they told you during discovery.
"You mentioned you wanted to do [X] sessions a week — if you were to continue, were you leaning towards [product] or [product]?"
"Based on everything you've told me, it sounds like [goal] is still the priority — how are you feeling about continuing to work towards that?"
"You've done [X] sessions now — from what you've experienced, do you feel like this is something you want to keep going with?"
"On a scale of 1–10, where are you sitting with continuing after the Intro Offer?"
"From what you've told me about [goal] and wanting to train [X] times a week — does continuing make sense for you?"

Complete discovery first, then use these questions to bridge to conversion.

"How are you feeling about continuing?"

Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"

3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
2/week2/Week Membership vs Class Pack

Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"Of those two options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
