Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
ENA3 Final SMS — Send if no answer on Call 3
Hi {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG calling. Tried to reach out but haven't been able to connect - hope all is well! Just checking in one last time - how are you feeling about continuing from here?
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
expired call 3 — no answer — no voicemail — ena3 final sms sent — moved to exp cna 3 then abandoned
expired call 3 — answered — conversion conversation — follow up set
converted — membership purchased — moved to sold
11
Expired (Call No Answer 3)
All three attempts exhausted. Move immediately to Abandoned.
ABANDONED ▼
About this stage
This stage is a pass-through only. ENA3 Final SMS has been sent. Drag to this stage and then immediately drag to Abandoned — no need to wait.
ℹ️
If the client replies at any point after being moved to Abandoned — revert status to Open via activity feed and move card to the appropriate stage.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
no response after all attempts — moved to abandoned
12
Future Follow Up
Client requested follow up at a later date. Call on task date and run full sales process.
HOLDING ▼
What to do
  1. Right-click client name → open in new tab → call and SMS from here
  2. Check notes — review discovery and any previous interactions
  3. Call the client on the task date
  4. No answer → leave voicemail → send IOFUP1 SMS → set task for next shift
  5. Next shift → call again · no voicemail → send IOFUP2 Final SMS → drag to Abandoned
  6. Answer → complete discovery if not done · run options, recommendation and close
  7. Move card to the stage that reflects the outcome if required
IOFUP1 SMS — Send if no answer on first call
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} from STRONG — just tried to give you a call to follow up from our last chat. Feel free to call or text back whenever suits you. Otherwise I'll try again soon.
IOFUP2 Final SMS — Send if no answer on second call
Hey {{contact.first_name}}, {{custom_values.fromname_member}} here from STRONG. Just a quick final follow up — if the timing isn't right, no worries at all. But if you're ready to move forward or want to pick up the chat, just reach out. All the best.
Call Script — Future Follow Up
1
Opener
"Hey [Name], it's [Your Name] from STRONG — just calling to follow up from our last chat. [Brief reference to what was discussed.] Just wanted to pick up from there."
Pick up exactly where you left off. Check your notes before calling.
2
Discovery 5–10 min
Q1"What made you look into STRONG?"
Q2"Are you doing any training at the moment?"
Q3"Has anything stopped you from starting before?"
Q4"How many sessions a week would you ideally want to do?"
Q5"When were you thinking of getting started?"
Minimum before moving on: goal · frequency · start date.
3
Options 2–3 min
Two options from their answers. Present neutrally — no recommendation yet.
2/week2/Week Membership vs Class Pack
3+/weekUnlimited Membership vs 2/Week Membership
FlexibleClass Pack vs 2/Week Membership
4
Recommendation
Pick one. Say it directly. One reason tied to what they told you.
"Of those two options, I'd recommend [recommendation tied to what they told you]. How does that sound? Do you have any questions?"
5
Objections
Acknowledge → Explore → Address → Reconfirm.
"Does that help? Is there anything else holding you back?"
6
Close
Mirror ask— confirm [Product] + timing in their own words, then ask.
"Okay, so just confirming — you're leaning towards the [Product], wanting to start [Date]. Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Momentum close— when momentum is already there, skip the recap and ask if they're happy to proceed.
"Are you happy to get that sorted now, or was there anything else you wanted to cover?"
Alternate close— use for clarity, affirmation, or momentum — product alt, start date alt, or both.
Product: "Are you happy to get the [Product] sorted, or would you prefer to start with the [Product]?"
Start date: "Did you want to get started this week or next?"
RULE OF SILENCE — ASK. STOP TALKING. WAIT. DON'T FILL THE GAP.
Committed → guide client purchase through app or submit order via Core > complete 2 step process > confirm order complete on Core
Not ready → lock in a specific callback date and time before hanging up.
Further Reading
📚
Keep SharpeningReview these sections to strengthen your call
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Call Notes
follow up call 1 — no answer — voicemail left — iofup1 sms sent — task set next shift
follow up call 2 — no answer — no voicemail — iofup2 final sms sent — moved to abandoned
follow up call — answered — conversion conversation — follow up set
converted — membership purchased — moved to sold
discovery notes updated
Discovery Notes
📋
Discovery Call NotesCopies full template — paste into Hapana call notes
13
Membership / Package
Client has converted to a membership or class pack. Pipeline complete — Won.
CONVERTED ▼
What to do
  1. Confirm membership or class pack purchase is processed in Core
  2. Confirm first class is booked under their new membership or pack
  3. Drag card to Won status
  4. Log outcome note
🎉
Great work — they're in. Make sure the purchase is confirmed in Core before closing the card. Confirm their first class is booked so there's no drop-off.
Quick-copy notes for Hapana Grow. Tap any note to copy it, then paste into the client's call notes field.
Outcome Notes
converted — membership purchased — first class booked — moved to won
converted — class pack purchased — first class booked — moved to won
Chapter 04 — Scenarios & Objections / Leads Scenarios

Leads Scenarios

Reference Leads Pipeline
When to use this section

You've completed discovery and you know the person's profile. This section gives you the options to present and the recommendation to land for each common scenario on a Leads Pipeline call. Find the scenario that matches your client, follow the flow, and close.

Jump to scenario
01Motivated, ready to start
02Beginner, nervous
03Budget-conscious
04Training elsewhere
05Fell off, getting back in
06Injuries / limitations
07Bad experience elsewhere
08Wants unlimited, skip Intro
09Only 1 session/week
10Start after a specific date
01
Motivated, ready to start
Clear goal, 2–3+ sessions/week, ready to go, no major barriers
▼
Profile: Clear goal (tone up, get stronger, lose weight). Wants to train 2–3+ times a week. Ready to start soon. No major barriers.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. You could do a casual session — that's $35, you just book in and come try a class. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which lets you try a few different class types and see what works for you."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"Of those two, I'd go with the Intro Offer. 5 sessions over 14 days gives you enough time to actually feel the difference, try a few different class types, and figure out what works for you. At $50 it's the lowest-risk way to get started properly. How does that sound?"
02
Beginner, nervous, uncertain
New to Pilates, nervous about walking into a class, barrier is intimidation
▼
Profile: New to Pilates. Nervous about walking into a class. Maybe hasn't exercised in a while. Barrier is intimidation, not budget or time.
Options — Intro Offer vs Starter Class
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. There's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which gives you a proper feel for it. Or if you'd rather ease in first, we run a free Starter Class every Saturday at 11am — it's specifically designed for beginners so you're not walking into a full class cold."
Recommendation — Starter Class
"Given everything you've told me, I'd actually start with the Starter Class. It's free, it's specifically designed so you learn the equipment and get comfortable with the movements before jumping into a full session. By the end of it you'll know exactly whether this is for you — before spending a cent. Does that feel like the right starting point?"
03
Budget-conscious but interested
Price is a concern, wants to try it without overspending
▼
Profile: Interested but price is a concern. May have mentioned cost as a barrier in discovery. Wants to try it but doesn't want to overspend.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. You could do a casual session — that's $35 for a single class. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd go with the Intro Offer over the casual. You're getting 5 sessions for $50 — that's $10 a class — compared to $35 for a single session. And you've got 14 days to use them, so there's no pressure to rush. It's by far the better value if you're thinking about doing more than one class. Does that make sense?"
04
Currently training elsewhere
Already active, looking to add STRONG alongside existing training
▼
Profile: Already active — gym, CrossFit, yoga, running, whatever. Looking to add STRONG to their routine, not replace what they're doing.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to try it out. You could do a casual session — that's $35, just book in and see how it feels alongside what you're already doing. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which gives you a proper chance to see how it fits with your routine."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd go with the Intro Offer. Since you're already training, 5 sessions over 14 days gives you enough time to work it around your current schedule and see where it fits. You'll get to try different class types too — some people find the Rowformer complements what they're already doing more than the Bikeformer, or the other way around. At $50 it's a pretty low-risk way to test it. How does that sound?"
05
Fell off, getting back in
Was active, fell off, wants to restart but hesitant about committing
▼
Profile: Was active at some point. Fell off for whatever reason (life, injury, motivation). Wants to get back into it but might be hesitant about committing.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to get back into it. You could do a casual session — that's $35, just come try a class and see how you feel. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which gives you a chance to ease back in without any long-term commitment."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd go with the Intro Offer. Getting back into it after a break can feel like a big step — 5 sessions over 14 days gives you enough time to build some momentum without overwhelming yourself. And there's no commitment beyond the $50, so if life gets in the way again you're not locked into anything. How does that sound?"
06
Injuries / physical limitations
Has an injury or limitation, worried about whether they can do it safely
▼
Profile: Has a specific injury, condition, or physical limitation. Interested but worried about whether they can actually do it safely.
Options — Intro Offer vs Starter Class
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. There's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which gives you enough time to see how your body responds. Or if you'd rather start slower, we run a free Starter Class every Saturday at 11am where you'll learn the equipment and movements at a comfortable pace."
Recommendation — Intro Offer + Starter Class first
"I'd go with the Intro Offer — but what I'd suggest is come to the Starter Class first. It's free, it's on Saturday at 11am, and it's a slower pace so you can learn the equipment and see how everything feels with your [injury/limitation]. Then when you're comfortable, you start your Intro Offer sessions knowing exactly what to expect. Your 14 days don't kick in until you book your first Intro Offer class, so there's no rush. How does that sound?"
07
Bad experience elsewhere
Previous negative experience at another gym or studio, guarded
▼
Profile: Tried somewhere else before and had a negative experience — pushy sales, felt out of place, didn't enjoy it, got injured, whatever. Interested in STRONG but guarded.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to try it out. You could do a casual session — that's $35, just come in and see if it feels different. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which gives you a proper chance to experience it before deciding anything."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd go with the Intro Offer. 5 sessions gives you enough time to actually get a feel for what STRONG is like, and one class isn't always enough to know. At $50 for 5 sessions there's not a lot of risk, and if it's not for you, that's it. No contracts, no pressure. How does that sound?"
08
Wants unlimited, skip Intro
High motivation, wants to commit to membership straight away
▼
Profile: High motivation. Already decided they want to commit. Has asked for unlimited or a membership directly. No need to sell the concept — just need to land the right product.
💡
Don't redirect to Intro Offer. If they're ready to commit, sign them up. Discovery still happens to confirm the right product.
Options — Unlimited no-contract ($75/wk) vs Unlimited 6-month ($70/wk)
"So there's two ways to do the Unlimited. There's the no-contract option at $75 a week — unlimited classes, no lock-in. Or there's the 6-month membership at $70 a week — same unlimited access, and that rate locks in for the life of your membership, so even after the 6 months it just rolls over at the same price. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — if committed long-term
"I'd go with the 6-month. You've said you want to train [frequency] and you're ready to go — the $70 a week is the better rate and that price stays with you for as long as you keep your membership. How does that sound?"
Recommendation — if any hesitation about locking in
"I'd go with the no-contract. It's $75 a week but you've got full flexibility — if anything changes you can cancel without worrying about a contract. How does that sound?"
09
Only 1 session per week
Low frequency, could be schedule, budget, or testing the waters
▼
Profile: Interested but only wants to come once a week. Could be schedule, budget, or just testing the waters. Low frequency.
Options — Class pack vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. If you know you just want to do one session a week, there's class packs — the 5-pack is $159 and gives you 30 days. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days, which is a good way to try it out."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd start with the Intro Offer. A lot of people come in thinking once a week and end up wanting to come more once they get into it. 5 sessions over 14 days lets you figure out what your rhythm actually is before committing to a pack. And at $50 it's cheaper than the 5-pack anyway. How does that sound?"
10
Start after a specific date
Interested and committed, but has a holiday, event, or reason to delay
▼
Profile: Interested and committed, but has a holiday, event, or reason to delay their start. Not a hesitation issue — genuine timing.
Options — Casual vs Intro Offer
"So there's a couple of ways to get started. You could do a casual session — that's $35, just book in when you're ready. Or there's the Intro Offer — 5 sessions for $50 over 14 days."
Recommendation — Intro Offer
"I'd go with the Intro Offer. Your 14 days don't start until you book your first class, so there's no rush on timing. What I can do is get you set up now and you just book your first session for after [their date] — that way you don't have to worry about calling back or remembering to do it later. Everything's ready when you are. How does that sound?"
Chapter 04 — Scenarios & Objections / Intro Offer Scenarios

Intro Offer Scenarios

Reference Intro Offer Pipeline
When to use this section

The client has completed their Intro Offer. Discovery has already been done — the bridge resurfaces what they told you and checks if anything has changed based on their actual experience. Find the scenario that matches, follow the flow, and close.

Jump to scenario
013+ sessions/week → Unlimited
022 sessions/week → Membership
03Needs flexibility → Class pack
04Only 1 session/week
05Cost concern
06Schedule clash
07Already training elsewhere
08Not sure they'll stick to it
09Didn't use enough sessions
10Wants to continue, not right now
11Injuries, still uncertain
12Loved it, casual only
13Found it too difficult
14Didn't get enough help in class
01
3+ sessions/week → Unlimited
High frequency during trial, enjoying it, said 3+ sessions/week in discovery
▼
Profile: High frequency during trial. Enjoying it. Discovery done — said 3+ sessions/week.
Bridge
"You mentioned you wanted to train around [X] times a week — has that changed at all since you started, or are you still feeling good at that pace?"
Options — Unlimited no-contract ($75/wk) vs Unlimited 6-month ($70/wk)
"So there's two ways to keep going at that pace. There's the no-contract option at $75 a week — unlimited classes, no lock-in. Or there's the 6-month membership at $70 a week — same unlimited access, and that rate locks in for the life of your membership, so even after the 6 months it just rolls over at the same price. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Unlimited 6-month
"Of those two, I'd go with the 6-month. You've been coming [X] times a week and you're clearly enjoying it — the $70 a week is the better rate and that price stays with you for as long as you keep your membership. How does that sound?"
02
2 sessions/week → Membership
Consistent at twice a week through the trial, happy with that rhythm
▼
Profile: Consistent at twice a week through the trial. Happy with that rhythm. Not looking to do more.
Bridge
"You've been coming about twice a week through the Intro Offer — is that the kind of rhythm you'd want to keep up?"
Options — Class pack vs 2/week membership ($50/wk)
"So there's a couple of ways to do that. If you didn't want a weekly commitment, there's class packs — but they're really more for people who can't commit to a regular schedule. Or there's the 2/week membership at $50 a week, no contract — you just keep going at the pace you're at. Does that make sense so far?"
Recommendation — 2/week membership
"I'd go with the membership. It works out better value per class than a pack, there's no big upfront cost, and you've already found your rhythm at twice a week so it just keeps that going. Does that work for you?"
03
Needs flexibility → Class pack
Irregular schedule during trial, shift work/travel, can't commit to set frequency
▼
Profile: Irregular schedule during trial. Shift work, travel, or lifestyle that doesn't suit a weekly membership. Enjoyed it but can't commit to a set frequency.
Bridge
"How's the scheduling been working for you — have you been able to get to classes when you want to?"
Options — 2/week membership ($50/wk) vs Class pack
"So there's a couple of options. The 2/week membership is $50 a week, no contract — gives you a set rhythm each week. Or there's a class pack where you buy sessions and use them whenever it suits — the 10-pack is $299 and gives you 60 days. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Class pack
"I'd go with the pack. With your schedule it gives you the flexibility to book when you can without paying for weeks you can't make it. And there's no commitment beyond the pack itself. How does that sound?"
04
Only 1 session per week
Coming once a week through the trial, might increase later
▼
Profile: Coming once a week through the trial. Might increase later but right now that's where they're at.
Bridge
"You've been coming about once a week — is that where you want to sit for now, or do you think you'd want to bump that up?"
Options — Casual ($35/session) vs 10-class pack ($299/60 days)
"So if once a week is the go, there's a couple of ways to do it. You can do casual at $35 a session and just book as you go. Or there's the 10-class pack at $299 which gives you 60 days — works out to about $30 a class. Does that make sense so far?"
Recommendation — 10-class pack
"I'd go with the 10-pack. You're saving $5 a class compared to casual and you've got 60 days to use them. And if you end up wanting to come more, you've got the sessions there. That's about $50 saved overall too. How does that sound?"
05
Cost concern
Enjoyed the IO but worried about ongoing cost, barrier is price not interest
▼
Profile: Enjoyed the Intro Offer but worried about the ongoing cost of a membership. Liked the experience, the barrier is price not interest.
Bridge
"How have you found the sessions so far — has it been what you expected?"
[Let them respond positively, then:]
"And if the pricing worked for you, is continuing something you'd want to do?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership (based on frequency from discovery)
"So there's a couple of ways to keep going. There's class packs where you buy a set of sessions upfront and use them within a timeframe — no weekly cost. Or there's the [2/week at $50 / Unlimited at $70] membership which is a weekly payment, no big upfront cost. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Membership (matched to their frequency)
"I'd go with the membership. At $[50/70] a week it's actually the better value per class than a pack, and because it's weekly there's no big lump sum — it just ticks over. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "That's still a lot per week."
"I get it. If you think about it though, at $[50/70] a week for [2/unlimited] sessions, that's $[25/23] a class — cheaper than most coffees and lunches. And there's no contract on the [2/week / no-contract unlimited], so if it ever stops working for you, you're not locked in. Does that change how it feels?"
06
Schedule clash
Enjoyed the IO but struggled to get to classes, work/family timetable clash
▼
Profile: Enjoyed the Intro Offer but struggled to get to the classes they wanted. Work hours, family commitments, or timetable doesn't line up with their availability.
Bridge
"How's the scheduling been for you — have you been able to get to the classes you want?"
[Let them explain the clash, then:]
"If you could find a rhythm that worked, would continuing be something you'd want to do?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership (based on frequency from discovery)
"So there's a couple of ways to look at it. A class pack lets you buy sessions and use them when you can — no pressure to come a set number of times each week. Or if you can find a rhythm that works, the [2/week at $50 / Unlimited at $70] membership gives you better value per class. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — if they can still make 1–2 sessions consistently
"I'd still go with the membership. It doesn't have to be the same classes every week — the main thing is finding a couple of anchor sessions that work for your schedule and building the routine around those. Regardless of class type, you're still getting the benefit every time you show up. How does that sound?"
Recommendation — if the clash is significant
"I'd go with a class pack for now. The 10-pack is $299 and gives you 60 days — so you just book in when your schedule allows and there's no pressure if a week doesn't work out. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I just wish there were more class times that worked for me."
"I get that. But even if the exact times aren't perfect, the main thing is getting a minimum number of sessions in each week and building the habit. Try to find a couple of anchor sessions that you can commit to — it doesn't matter what class type, you're still benefiting from every session. Worst case, you try it for a couple of months and if it's not working you can cancel with 21 days notice — or if you're on a pack, just don't repurchase. Does that make sense?"
07
Already training elsewhere
Did the IO alongside existing gym/training, not sure about paying for two
▼
Profile: Did the Intro Offer alongside their existing gym or training. Enjoyed STRONG but not sure about paying for two things at once.
Bridge
"You mentioned you're still doing [their current training] — how have you found balancing the two?"
[Let them respond, then:]
"If it came down to it, could you see yourself replacing [current training] with STRONG, or is it more about doing both?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership
"So there's a couple of ways to look at it. A class pack means no weekly commitment on top of what you're already paying — you just buy sessions and use them when you want. Or if you want to make it a regular part of your routine, the 2/week membership at $50 a week keeps you coming consistently without a huge commitment. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — if they want it as a regular addition
"I'd go with the 2/week. Two sessions a week fits nicely alongside what you're already doing and you're getting better value per class than a pack. How does that sound?"
Recommendation — if they're not sure how often they'd come
"I'd go with a class pack. The 10-pack is $299 and gives you 60 days — means you can fit STRONG in around your other training without a second weekly payment stacking up. How does that sound?"
Recommendation — if they're open to making STRONG their main thing
"I'd go with the Unlimited. If you're thinking about shifting your training over to STRONG, the Unlimited at $70 a week gives you the best value and you can drop what you're paying at [other gym]. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I just can't justify paying for two memberships."
"Totally fair. That's why the pack might be the better option for now — no weekly cost, you just buy the sessions and use them. And if you ever decide to shift more of your training to STRONG, you can always switch to a membership later. Does that work?"
08
Not sure they'll stick to it
Enjoyed the IO but has a history of starting things and dropping off
▼
Profile: Enjoyed the Intro Offer but has a history of starting things and dropping off. Worried about committing to something they might not maintain.
Bridge
"How have you found the consistency through the Intro Offer — have you been able to keep up with it?"
[Let them respond, then:]
"What do you think would be the thing that makes you stick to it this time?"
Options — Class pack vs 2/week membership ($50/wk)
"So there's a couple of ways to keep going. A class pack lets you buy sessions without a weekly commitment — if you drop off, you're not paying for something you're not using. Or the 2/week membership is $50 a week, no contract, and you can cancel with 21 days notice if it's not working. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — 2/week membership
"I'd go with the membership. Having it there every week actually helps build the routine — it's harder to stay consistent when you're buying sessions one pack at a time. And because there's no contract, if it ever stops working you just give 21 days notice and you're done. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "Yeah but that's what I always say and then I stop coming."
"I hear you. But the fact that you came through the Intro Offer and you're still here having this conversation says something. The membership gives you the structure to keep showing up — and if it doesn't work out, you're not locked in. Worst case you try it for a couple of months and cancel. How does that feel?"
09
Loved it but didn't use enough sessions
Only came 2–3 times out of 5, enjoyed it but feels they can't judge yet
▼
Profile: Only came 2–3 times out of their 5 sessions. Enjoyed what they did but feels like they can't make a proper decision yet.
Bridge
"You got to a couple of sessions — how did you find them?"
[Let them respond, then:]
"Do you feel like you got enough of a feel for it, or would you have liked more time?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership (based on frequency from discovery)
"So there's a couple of ways to keep going. A class pack lets you buy sessions and take your time — no weekly commitment, you just book when you're ready. Or if you're feeling good about it, the [2/week at $50 / Unlimited at $70] membership gets you into a routine straight away. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Class pack
"I'd go with a pack for now. The 10-pack gives you 60 days, so you've got plenty of time to get a proper feel for it without any pressure. And if you end up loving it and want to come more regularly, you can always switch to a membership later. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I just feel like I didn't get to try it properly."
"That's exactly what the pack is for. You're not committing to anything long-term — you're just giving yourself more time to experience it properly. And you've already done a few sessions so you're not starting from scratch. How does that feel?"
10
Wants to continue but not right now
Enjoyed the IO, has something coming up — holiday, work period, not a commitment issue
▼
Profile: Enjoyed the Intro Offer. Wants to keep going but has something coming up — holiday, work period, family event. Not a commitment issue, genuine timing.
Bridge
"How have you found the sessions overall?"
[Let them respond, then:]
"And once [their event] is done, is continuing something you'd want to do?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership (based on frequency from discovery)
"So there's a couple of ways to pick it back up. A class pack lets you buy sessions now and start using them whenever you're ready — the clock doesn't start until your first booking. Or if you know you want to get into a routine after [their event], the [2/week at $50 / Unlimited at $70] membership can start whenever you want. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Membership
"I'd go with the membership. We can set the start date for after [their event] so you're not paying for weeks you're not here. That way it's sorted, you don't have to think about it, and when you're back you just book in and go. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I'd rather just sort it out when I'm back."
"Totally up to you. The reason I'd suggest doing it now is just so it's done — you don't have to remember to call back or go through it all again. But if you'd prefer to wait, I can set a follow-up for after [their date] and give you a call then. Which works better for you?"
11
Injuries/limitations, still uncertain
Has an injury or limitation, tried the IO but not sure STRONG is sustainable long-term
▼
Profile: Has an injury or physical limitation. Did the Intro Offer and it was okay, but still not sure if STRONG is sustainable for their body long-term.
Bridge
"How did your body feel through the sessions — were the instructors able to work around your [injury/limitation]?"
[Let them respond, then:]
"If you felt comfortable enough to keep going, would that be something you'd want to do?"
Options — Class pack vs Membership (based on frequency from discovery)
"So there's a couple of ways to keep going. A class pack lets you buy sessions and go at your own pace — no weekly commitment, so if you need to take a week off for your body there's no pressure. Or if you're feeling confident, the [2/week at $50 / Unlimited at $70] membership gets you into a routine. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — Class pack
"I'd go with a pack for now. The 10-pack gives you 60 days, so you can ease into it at your own pace and see how your body responds over a longer stretch. And if it's all feeling good, you can always move to a membership later. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I'm just worried I'll pay for something and then not be able to use it."
"That's fair. The good thing with the pack is there's no weekly cost ticking over — you've got 60 days and you just book when you're feeling good. And our instructors can modify anything that doesn't feel right, so you're never stuck doing a movement that doesn't work for you. Does that help?"
12
Loved it but casual only
Enjoyed the IO, happy with the experience, but doesn't want any ongoing commitment
▼
Profile: Enjoyed the Intro Offer. Happy with the experience. But doesn't want any kind of ongoing commitment — no membership, no pack. Just wants to drop in when they feel like it.
Bridge
"How did you find the Intro Offer overall — did you enjoy it?"
[Let them respond positively, then:]
"And is continuing something you'd want to do, even if it's just when it suits you?"
Options — Casual ($35/session) vs 10-class pack ($299/60 days)
"So there's a couple of ways to do that. You can do casual at $35 a session and just book in whenever you want. Or there's the 10-class pack at $299 which gives you 60 days — works out to about $30 a class and you still just book when it suits you. Does that make sense?"
Recommendation — 10-class pack
"I'd go with the 10-pack. You're still booking when you want with no weekly commitment — the only difference is you're saving about $5 a class. That's about $50 saved overall too. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I'd rather just pay as I go."
"Totally fine. Casual is always there — $35 a session, no commitment, you just book in when you want. The pack is just there if you ever want to save a bit per class. Either way you're welcome anytime. Does that work?"
13
Found it too difficult
Completed some/all of the IO but found classes harder than expected
▼
Profile: Completed some or all of the Intro Offer but found the classes harder than expected. Feeling like it might not be for them because of the intensity.
Bridge
"How did you find the sessions overall — were they what you expected?"
[Let them explain it was tough, then:]
"What part of it felt the hardest for you?"
Address the concern
"So the first few sessions are always the hardest — that's completely normal. Your body is learning new movements and adjusting to the equipment. Most people find it gets noticeably easier by session 4 or 5. And our instructors can always modify anything that feels too much — you just let them know at the start of class."
[Trial close:] "Does that make sense?" [If open to continuing, move to product options based on frequency. If still hesitant:]
Recommendation — Class pack
"I'd go with a pack for now. The 10-pack gives you 60 days so there's no pressure — you can ease into it at your own pace and see how it feels as your body adjusts. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I just don't think it's for me."
"That's fair. But from what you've described it sounds like it was more about being new to it than it not being right for you. Most people who felt the same way in their first few sessions are the ones who end up loving it once their body catches up. A pack lets you test that without any commitment. Does that feel like it's worth a try?"
14
Didn't get enough help in class
Felt lost during classes, didn't get enough guidance from instructor
▼
Profile: Completed some or all of the Intro Offer but felt lost during classes. Didn't get enough guidance from the instructor. Might feel like they were left to figure it out on their own.
Bridge
"How did you find the sessions — did you feel comfortable with the equipment and movements?"
[Let them explain they felt lost or didn't get enough help, then:]
"I'm sorry to hear that. Which classes were you going to?"
[Listen — this helps identify if it was a specific instructor, class type, or time slot.]
Address the concern
"That's not the experience we want for you. Our instructors are there to guide you through every session, especially when you're new. What I'd suggest is trying a different class time — some classes are smaller and you'll get more one-on-one attention. And if you let the instructor know at the start that you're still learning the equipment, they'll keep a closer eye on you."
[Trial close:] "Does that help?" [If open to continuing, move to product options based on frequency. If still hesitant:]
Recommendation — Class pack
"I'd go with a pack for now. The 10-pack gives you 60 days, so you've got time to try a few different class times and find the ones that work best for you. No pressure, and if you're still not getting the experience you want, you're not locked into anything. How does that sound?"
Expected pushback — "I just don't want to pay for something and have the same experience."
"Totally fair. Here's what I'd do — book into a smaller class, let the instructor know you're still getting the hang of things, and see how that goes. If it's still not right, you've got sessions left on the pack and no commitment beyond that. Does that feel reasonable?"
Chapter 04 — Scenarios & Objections / General Objections

General Objections

Reference Both Pipelines
When to use this section

These are decision blockers, not product-fit concerns. They apply across both pipelines. When someone stalls, deflects, or raises a barrier that isn't about which product is right for them, this is where you go. Find the objection, follow the handling, and keep the conversation moving.

Jump to objection
01"I need to think about it"
02"I need to talk to my partner"
03"I'll sort it out later"
04Discount / competitor pricing
05"I don't like contracts"
06"I've seen bad reviews"
07"My friend goes to [competitor]"
01
"I need to think about it"
Stall — usually a polite exit, something unresolved underneath
▼
What's really going on
This is rarely about thinking. It's usually a polite way to end the conversation without committing. Something is unresolved — price, timing, confidence — but they haven't voiced it.
How to handle
"Of course — out of curiosity, which part of it did you want to have a think over?"
[Pause. Let them answer. Whatever they say is the real objection — handle that.]
If they can't name anything specific:
"No worries. Would you say it's more the price, the timing, or something else?"
[Whatever they name, address it directly using the relevant approach from Leads or Intro Offer Scenarios.]
If they genuinely just want time:
"No problem at all. How about I give you a call in a couple of days — does [day] work?"
[Lock in a specific callback. Never leave it open-ended.]
02
"I need to talk to my partner"
Deferral — sometimes genuine shared finances, sometimes a shield
▼
What's really going on
Sometimes genuine — shared finances, household decisions. But often it's a way to defer the decision without saying no. The key is to find out whether the partner is a real blocker or a shield.
How to handle
"Of course — is it more of a financial decision you make together, or were you wanting their opinion on whether it's right for you?"
If financial — respect it, but keep momentum:
"Totally get that. Is there anything I can help with in terms of the pricing or options so you've got everything you need for that conversation?"
[Then lock in a callback:]
"How about I give you a call on [day] — that gives you a chance to chat about it and I can answer any questions that come up?"
If opinion-based:
"From what you've told me, it sounds like this is something you're keen on — would it help if I sent you a quick summary of what we discussed and the recommendation so you can talk it through?"
[Lock in a callback. Never leave it open-ended.]
03
"I'll just sign up online / sort it out later"
Self-service deflection — wants to end the call, most won't come back
▼
What's really going on
They want to get off the phone. They might intend to sign up, but most people who say this don't come back. The longer between the conversation and the action, the less likely it happens.
How to handle
"For sure — you can definitely do that. I can also get it sorted for you right now over the phone if you wanted, just takes a couple of minutes. Would you prefer to do it now and get it sorted, or in your own time?"
[If they want to do it now — close.]
If they still want to do it themselves:
"No problem at all. Just so you know, if you have any questions when you're going through it, you can call or text me on this number and I'll walk you through it."
[Then lock in a follow-up:]
"I'll give you a call in about a week just to make sure you got started okay and see if you need anything. Does that work?"
04
Discount request / competitor pricing
Buying signal — testing for a better price or comparing to cheaper alternatives
▼
What's really going on
They want to buy — they're testing whether there's a better price. Or they're comparing to a cheaper alternative. Either way, this is a buying signal. The question is whether price is their real deciding factor or just a negotiation habit.
Step 1 — Find out if price is the real issue
"Out of curiosity — is getting the lowest price the main thing driving your decision, or is it more about finding the right fit?"
If price is genuinely their main consideration:
"Look, I'll be straight with you — STRONG isn't the cheapest option out there, and we're probably not going to be. If the lowest price is the main thing you're looking for, there are other places that will be cheaper, and I'm sure they're great for what they offer."
[Pause. Then bring it back to their goals:]
"But from what you've told me about wanting to [their goal from discovery], the reason STRONG works is because of what you get — the equipment, the programming, the coaching, the community. That's what's going to get you to where you want to be. And from what you've experienced so far, you've seen that. Does the value of what you're getting feel worth it?"
[If they agree the value is there — move to incentive sequence.]
Step 2 — Incentive sequence
If they're on board but still want a better deal:
"The [product] is $[price] a week — that's the standard rate. But I'll tell you what, I can offer you the first week free so you can get started without any cost upfront. How does that sound?"
If they push further:
"I can also do $[discounted rate] a week — that's a locked-in rate for the life of your membership. How does that sound?"
If still hesitant:
"Best I can do is the first week free and $[discounted rate] a week ongoing. That's the best rate we offer. Does that work for you?"
If they push beyond that:
"That's genuinely the best we can do. Does that work?"
If they're asking about the Intro Offer or class packs:
"That's already our best price on that — there's no discount on top of it."
05
"I don't like contracts"
Contract resistance — bad past experience, doesn't realise no-contract options exist
▼
What's really going on
They've had a bad experience with contracts before — gym, phone, whatever. The word "contract" triggers resistance regardless of what's actually being offered. Most of the time they don't realise how many no-contract options exist.
How to handle
"I get that — no one likes feeling locked in. The good news is most of what we offer doesn't have a contract at all. The 2/week membership, the no-contract Unlimited, and all the class packs — none of those have a lock-in. You can cancel with 21 days notice or just not repurchase a pack."
[Trial close:] "Does that change how you're feeling about it?" [If relieved and open — go back to recommendation based on their profile.]
If they ask about the 6-month:
"The only one with a contract is the 6-month Unlimited at $70 a week — and that's because it's the lowest rate we offer. After the 6 months it just rolls over at the same price with no lock-in. But if that's not for you, the no-contract Unlimited is $75 a week — same access, you just pay $5 more for the flexibility."
If they're still resistant to any commitment:
"A class pack might be the best starting point then. You buy sessions, use them within the timeframe, and there's nothing ongoing. You can always move to a membership later if you want to. How does that sound?"
06
"I've seen bad reviews"
Doubt from something they read online — interest is still there
▼
What's really going on
They've read something negative online and it's created doubt. They're interested enough to still be in the conversation, so the interest is there — they just need reassurance.
How to handle
"I appreciate you bringing that up. Out of curiosity, what did you see?"
[Let them tell you. Listen. Don't get defensive.]
If it's about a specific experience (instructor, class, cleanliness, etc.):
"I'm sorry to hear someone had that experience. I can't speak for what happened in that situation, but what I can tell you is that's not the standard we hold ourselves to. From what you've experienced yourself — how did you find it?"
[Bring it back to their own experience. Their experience is more relevant than a stranger's review.]
If it's general negativity or vague:
"There's always going to be mixed reviews for any business. The best way to judge is your own experience — and from what you've told me, it sounds like you've had a good one. Does that match up?"
If they haven't tried it yet (Leads):
"I'd say the best way to find out is to try it yourself. That's exactly what the Intro Offer is for — 5 sessions for $50 and you can make your own call. How does that sound?"
07
"My friend goes to [competitor]"
Social influence — pulled toward another studio by recommendation or familiarity
▼
What's really going on
Social influence. Their friend is at another studio and they're being pulled in that direction — either by recommendation, wanting to train together, or just familiarity. The objection isn't about STRONG being wrong, it's about something else feeling safer.
How to handle
"Got it — what are they doing over there?"
[Let them talk. Find out what the friend does, what they like about it, and whether training together is the draw.]
If it's about training together:
"Makes sense — training with a friend is great for accountability. Have you thought about getting them to come try STRONG with you? They're welcome to do a casual session or the Intro Offer and see how they find it."
If it's about the friend recommending the other place:
"It sounds like it works well for them. The question is whether it's the right fit for what you're looking for. From what you've told me about [their goal from discovery], STRONG is built around [relevant point of difference — low-impact, Rowformer, coaching, programming]. Have you tried anywhere else or just been going off their recommendation?"
If they haven't tried the competitor:
"I'd say try both and make your own call. You've already experienced STRONG — go try [competitor] and see which one feels right for you. Does that sound fair?"
If they have tried both:
"So you've seen both — which one did you feel better in?"
[Let their own answer do the work.]
SETTINGS
v1.0
Location Configuration
Location
Bankstown
Penrith
Wetherill Park
Leppington
Castle Hill
Products & Prices
Prices shown are for Bankstown. Default = network-wide default. Override = Location-specific value.
Promotions
Add current promotions. Each promotion has its own Shown/Hidden toggle. The default Intro Offer card can be toggled independently — show both, promo only, or default only.
STRONG Intro Offer (default)
Show the standard STRONG Intro Offer card
Toggles
Discounts & Incentives active
Master switch for all discounts and incentives at this location. When off, none will display in the playbook regardless of individual product settings. Configure values per product using the Edit button above.
Sauna available
Show sauna pricing and entitlements in the playbook
Late cancel fee active
Fee applied within cancellation window
Pack Sale active
Show discounted prices on class packs. Enter % off or $ off below — only one can be active at a time.
Sale discount — fill one only
% OFF
OR
$ OFF
Starter Class — Class Times
Day and time pairs shown on the Starter Class callout. Add multiple if this location runs more than one session per week.
Passport — Included Locations
These locations appear as pills on the Passport membership card. Add any location name — not limited to your five studios.
Policies & Fees
hours
Management Access Only
These settings affect all staff using this playbook. Make changes carefully. Data is stored locally on this device.
Data Management
Reset & Restore
Enable All Incentives
Turns on incentives for every product at this location. Uses current values — default suggestions if never edited, or your saved overrides if you've customised them.
Reset Incentives to Default
Removes all incentive customisations for this location. All products revert to incentives off. Prices and other settings are not affected.
Reset Prices to Default
Removes all price customisations for this location. All products revert to system default pricing. Incentive settings and other configurations are not affected.
Reset Location to Default
Removes all customisations for this location — prices, incentives, toggles, and intro offer settings. The location will run entirely on system defaults. This cannot be undone.
Reset incentive rules for back to defaults? Prices, toggles and all other settings are not affected.
⚠ FULL LOCATION RESET
This will reset to factory defaults.
ALL settings will be wiped — prices, visibility, toggles, incentives, and policies.
This cannot be undone.
Reset all product prices and expiry for back to defaults? Incentives, toggles and all other settings are not affected.
Data is stored in your browser's local storage. Exporting creates a JSON file you can paste into another device or share with your team.
Enter management password to continue.
No password has been set yet. Set one in Change Password below, then try again. For now, access is granted.
Incorrect password. Try again.
Change Password
Set a password for the active location to protect management actions. Each location has its own independent password. If no password is set, all management actions are open.
Reset via Recovery Password
Enter the recovery password to clear the management password and set a new one.
